manualFeedback.summary: Manual playability review found major camera/framing and visibility issues. Progress UI and Job Complete were understandable, and no runtime instability was reported, but the build is not acceptable as a first playable yet.

uxChangesApplied: Added HUD controls text: WASD / Arrow Keys: Move and E: Recover nearby target.
Added Recover labels and marker spheres above all three recovery targets.
Added nearest-target highlight that changes the marker and label to Press E: Recover.
Changed recovered targets to green/dimmed visual state and Recovered label.
Kept Job Complete visible in the progress UI after all three recoveries.
Switched generated materials to URP-compatible shaders to remove magenta/pink missing-shader rendering.
Reframed the main camera with an orthographic centered view so the player and recovery targets are visible.
Added a player marker/label and enlarged target markers/labels for readability.
Added a simple objective banner separate from the controls text.

reviewItems.question: Player can understand what to do.
reviewItems.finding: Controls are visible as text, but the user expects a more game-like tutorial/navigation pattern.

reviewItems.question: Movement can be judged.
reviewItems.finding: User could not judge movement feel because the player was not visible.

reviewItems.question: Camera framing supports play.
reviewItems.finding: User reported the camera did not show the player or goals clearly.

reviewItems.question: Three recovery targets are identifiable.
reviewItems.finding: User reported targets could not be evaluated because they were not visible in the camera view.

reviewItems.question: Recoverable state is visually clear.
reviewItems.finding: Press E text exists, but the user could not evaluate it due to camera/visibility issues.

reviewItems.question: E-key recovery response is satisfying.
reviewItems.finding: User said only text changes, so the response feels ambiguous.

reviewItems.question: Progress UI is readable.
reviewItems.finding: User said the UI can be understood, while noting it is not recommended as a normal game presentation.

reviewItems.question: Job Complete is clear.
reviewItems.finding: User said Job Complete is understandable.

reviewItems.question: No errors, freezes, or abnormal behavior.
Overall acceptable as a first playable.
reviewItems.finding: User said it is not acceptable because nothing meaningful was visible and it feels like a WASD/interaction test.

manualAcceptanceCriteria.summary: Within 3 seconds of starting play, the player understands the objective and controls.
manualAcceptanceCriteria.note: UI text exists, but the user noted this does not feel like a normal tutorial/navigation experience.

manualAcceptanceCriteria.summary: All three recovery targets are visually distinguishable on screen.
manualAcceptanceCriteria.note: User could not evaluate target visibility because the camera/framing made gameplay objects unreadable.

manualAcceptanceCriteria.summary: The target that can be recovered near the player is visually distinguished.
manualAcceptanceCriteria.note: User said interaction feedback is hard to evaluate because of the camera problem.

manualAcceptanceCriteria.summary: Recovering all targets clearly transitions the job to complete.
manualAcceptanceCriteria.note: User understood the progress UI and Job Complete text, while noting the presentation is not ideal for a normal game.

manualAcceptanceCriteria.summary: Manual play remains free of visible errors, freezes, or abnormal behavior.
manualAcceptanceCriteria.note: User reported no visible errors, freezes, or abnormal behavior. Automated console verification is tracked separately.

remainingPlayabilityIssues: Retest required after camera, material, player marker, target marker, and objective banner fixes.
The current experience still relies on prototype UI/text and does not yet provide a full game-like tutorial flow.
E-key recovery feedback is still minimal and should eventually receive animation/audio/VFX beyond this generated primitive slice.
User requested broader design improvement; this pass only applies minimal generated-folder UX fixes.
nextAction: Rerun the generated build and confirm whether camera framing, material colors, player visibility, and target visibility are now acceptable.
